data.body: STATUS 2026-07-12 (/rebar-implement): CLAIMED (in_progress), plan HARDENED + signed through the claim gate, NOT yet implemented. This is one of the two largest kernel leaves — Pass-2 grounding hardening, 3 components: (1) absence-claim veto — add abstain-safe binaries claims_absence + absence_confirmed_in_context to the SHARED verification contract (verify.py, alongside cited_reference_accurate at ~L143; NOT in GRADED_BINARY) + a symmetric deterministic veto in decide.py (mirror the cited-reference veto at decide.py:319-327: claims_absence==yes AND absence_confirmed_in_context==no -> dropped, reason veto:absence-refuted); (2) DET-tier hygiene backstop at the DET emission point (det_floor.det_blocking_findings/det_advisory_findings @932/942 or partition_findings @orchestrator.py:380 — NOT pass3_over_findings, which DET findings bypass; P5 task-dag ALREADY emits subjects so it is NOT a fix target); (3) operator-attested AC awareness — DET-detect [operator-attested] and inject operator_attested into severity_attributes in plan_review/workflow_ops.py plan_review_decide (~L324), clearing ac_unverifiable from attrs BEFORE impact_plan reads it (impact_plan stays byte-unchanged). Contract test tests/unit/test_review_kernel.py:176-207 must be updated to include the 2 new binary fields. f1fa 11:44 verdict (norm ne7642adbf79 + G3 n0eb3f2e9da9) becomes the regression fixture. REMAINING: implement under held-out TDD (RED-first: absence-veto drop + additive-contract + DET-hygiene drop + operator-attested ac_unverifiable-clear), commit in the stack, push, close.
